title: Public holidays in Brazil

infobox.value: [New Year's Day](/wiki/New_Year%27s_Day "New Year's Day") | *Ano Novo* | Celebrates the beginning of the year. Festivities include counting down to midnight starting from the preceding night. It also signals the traditional end of the holiday season.

[Tiradentes Day](/wiki/Tiradentes "Tiradentes") | *Dia de Tiradentes* | Anniversary of the death of [Tiradentes](/wiki/Tiradentes "Tiradentes") (1792), considered the national martyr for being part of the [Inconfidência Mineira](/wiki/Inconfid%C3%AAncia_Mineira "Inconfidência Mineira"), an insurgent movement that aimed to establish an independent Brazilian republic.
[Labour Day](/wiki/Labour_Day "Labour Day") | *Dia do Trabalho* | Celebrates the achievements of workers and the labour movement.

[Independence Day](/wiki/Independence_Day_(Brazil) "Independence Day (Brazil)") | *Dia da Independência* | Celebrates Brazil's [declaration of independence](/wiki/Brazilian_Declaration_of_Independence "Brazilian Declaration of Independence") from Portugal on September 7, 1822.

[Our Lady of Aparecida](/wiki/Our_Lady_of_Aparecida "Our Lady of Aparecida") | *Nossa Senhora Aparecida* | Commemorates the [Virgin Mary](/wiki/Virgin_Mary "Virgin Mary") as *[Nossa Senhora da Conceição Aparecida](/wiki/Our_Lady_of_Aparecida "Our Lady of Aparecida")*, [patron saint](/wiki/Patron_saint "Patron saint") of Brazil.
[All Souls' Day](/wiki/All_Souls%27_Day "All Souls' Day") | *Dia de Finados* | Christian holiday; it commemorates the faithful departed.

[Republic Day](/wiki/Proclamation_of_the_Republic_(Brazil) "Proclamation of the Republic (Brazil)") | *Proclamação da República* | Commemorates the end of the [Empire of Brazil](/wiki/Empire_of_Brazil "Empire of Brazil") and the proclamation of the [Brazilian Republic](/wiki/Brazilian_Republic "Brazilian Republic") on November 15, 1889.

[Black Consciousness Day](/wiki/Black_Awareness_Day "Black Awareness Day") | *Dia da Consciência Negra* | Celebrates the Black Consciousness Day in honor of [Zumbi dos Palmares](/wiki/Zumbi_dos_Palmares "Zumbi dos Palmares"), born this day (year is uncertain).
[Christmas Day](/wiki/Christmas_Day "Christmas Day") | *Natal* | Celebrates the [nativity of Jesus](/wiki/Nativity_of_Jesus "Nativity of Jesus").

* [Timeline](/wiki/Timeline_of_Brazilian_history "Timeline of Brazilian history") * [Indigenous peoples](/wiki/Indigenous_peoples_in_Brazil "Indigenous peoples in Brazil") * [Colonial Brazil (1500–1815)](/wiki/Colonial_Brazil "Co
Wednesday after Carnival, the last Carnival day.
is celebrated on the Thursday, 60 days after Easter Sunday.

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

For the album by Bud Shank, see [*Holiday in Brazil*](/wiki/Holiday_in_Brazil "Holiday in Brazil").

In [Brazil](/wiki/Brazil "Brazil"), public holidays may be legislated at the federal, statewide and municipal levels. Most holidays are observed nationwide.[[1]](#cite_note-1)

Apart from the yearly official holidays (listed below),[[2]](#cite_note-2)[[3]](#cite_note-3)[[4]](#cite_note-4) the [Constitution of Brazil](/wiki/Constitution_of_Brazil "Constitution of Brazil") also establishes that [election days](/wiki/Election_Day_(politics) "Election Day (politics)") are to be considered national holidays as well. [General elections](/wiki/General_election "General election") are biennially held on the first Sunday of October in the first round, and on the last Sunday of October in the second round. Numerous religious and ethnic holidays are also celebrated in Brazil.

| Date | English name | [Portuguese](/wiki/Portuguese_language "Portuguese language") name | Remarks |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| January 1 | [New Year's Day](/wiki/New_Year%27s_Day "New Year's Day") | *Ano Novo* | Celebrates the beginning of the year. Festivities include counting down to midnight starting from the preceding night. It also signals the traditional end of the holiday season. |
| April 21 | [Tiradentes Day](/wiki/Tiradentes "Tiradentes") | *Dia de Tiradentes* | Anniversary of the death of [Tiradentes](/wiki/Tiradentes "Tiradentes") (1792), considered the national martyr for being part of the [Inconfidência Mineira](/wiki/Inconfid%C3%AAncia_Mineira "Inconfidência Mineira"), an insurgent movement that aimed to establish an independent Brazilian republic. |
| May 1 | [Labour Day](/wiki/Labour_Day "Labour Day") | *Dia do Trabalho* | Celebrates the achievements of workers and the labour movement. |
| September 7 | [Independence Day](/wiki/Independence_Day_(Brazil) "Independence Day (Brazil)") | *Dia da Independência* | Celebrates Brazil's [declaration of independence](/wiki/Brazilian_Declaration_of_Independence "Brazilian Declaration of Independence") from Portugal on September 7, 1822. |
| October 12 | [Our Lady of Aparecida](/wiki/Our_Lady_of_Aparecida "Our Lady of Aparecida") | *Nossa Senhora Aparecida* | Commemorates the [Virgin Mary](/wiki/Virgin_Mary "Virgin Mary") as *[Nossa Senhora da Conceição Aparecida](/wiki/Our_Lady_of_Aparecida "Our Lady of Aparecida")*, [patron saint](/wiki/Patron_saint "Patron saint") of Brazil. |
| November 2 | [All Souls' Day](/wiki/All_Souls%27_Day "All Souls' Day") | *Dia de Finados* | Christian holiday; it commemorates the faithful departed. |
| November 15 | [Republic Day](/wiki/Proclamation_of_the_Republic_(Brazil) "Proclamation of the Republic (Brazil)") | *Proclamação da República* | Commemorates the end of the [Empire of Brazil](/wiki/Empire_of_Brazil "Empire of Brazil") and the proclamation of the [Brazilian Republic](/wiki/Brazilian_Republic "Brazilian Republic") on November 15, 1889. |
| November 20 | [Black Consciousness Day](/wiki/Black_Awareness_Day "Black Awareness Day") | *Dia da Consciência Negra* | Celebrates the Black Consciousness Day in honor of [Zumbi dos Palmares](/wiki/Zumbi_dos_Palmares "Zumbi dos Palmares"), born this day (year is uncertain). |
| December 25 | [Christmas Day](/wiki/Christmas_Day "Christmas Day") | *Natal* | Celebrates the [nativity of Jesus](/wiki/Nativity_of_Jesus "Nativity of Jesus"). |

## Secondary holidays

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Public_holidays_in_Brazil&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Secondary holidays")]

* **[Fat Tuesday](/wiki/Fat_Tuesday "Fat Tuesday")**, The day of [Carnival](/wiki/Carnival "Carnival").
* **[Ash Wednesday](/wiki/Ash_Wednesday "Ash Wednesday")** Wednesday after Carnival, the last Carnival day.
* **[Good Friday](/wiki/Good_Friday "Good Friday")**, the Friday preceding [Easter Sunday](/wiki/Easter_Sunday "Easter Sunday"), is a municipal holiday in many places.
* **[Corpus Christi](/wiki/Corpus_Christi_(feast) "Corpus Christi (feast)")** is celebrated on the Thursday, 60 days after Easter Sunday.
* **[Dia dos Namorados](/wiki/Dia_dos_Namorados "Dia dos Namorados")**, the [Brazilian](/wiki/Brazil "Brazil") equivalent of [Valentine's Day](/wiki/Valentine%27s_Day "Valentine's Day"), is observed on June 12. On this day, boyfriends and girlfriends, husbands and wives, exchange gifts, cards, or a flower bouquet. It is celebrated on the eve of [St. Anthony of Padua](/wiki/St._Anthony_of_Padua "St. Anthony of Padua")'s day, because, in Brazil, he is considered the patron of those who wants a loving companion.
* **[Festas Juninas](/wiki/Festa_Junina "Festa Junina")**, the Catholic feasts of [Santo Antônio](/wiki/St._Anthony_of_Padua "St. Anthony of Padua"), [São João](/wiki/Saint_John_the_Baptist "Saint John the Baptist") and [São Pedro](/wiki/Saint_Peter "Saint Peter") are celebrated June 13, 24, and 29 respectively in many Brazilian cities, especially in the [Northeast region](/wiki/Northeast_Region,_Brazil "Northeast Region, Brazil").
* Dia das Mães, the Brazilian observation of [Mother's Day](/wiki/Mother%27s_Day "Mother's Day"), is celebrated every second Sunday in May.
* Dia dos Pais, the Brazilian observation of [Father's Day](/wiki/Father%27s_Day "Father's Day"), is celebrated every second Sunday in August.
* Dia das Crianças, the Brazilian [Children's Day](/wiki/Children%27s_Day "Children's Day"), is celebrated on October 12, coinciding with the [Our Lady of Aparecida](/wiki/Our_Lady_of_Aparecida "Our Lady of Aparecida").

## Regional holidays

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Public_holidays_in_Brazil&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Regional holidays")]

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
|  | This list is [incomplete](/wiki/Wikipedia:WikiProject_Lists#Incomplete_lists "Wikipedia:WikiProject Lists"); you can help by [adding missing items](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Public_holidays_in_Brazil&action=edit). *(August 2008)* |

Federal law gives each state the right to create one state holiday, and each municipality to create up to four municipal holidays. Some of the more notable ones are:

* January 12: Founding of Belém, City of [Belém](/wiki/Bel%C3%A9m "Belém") only.
* January 20: [Saint Sebastian](/wiki/Saint_Sebastian "Saint Sebastian") (São Sebastião), City of [Rio de Janeiro](/wiki/Rio_de_Janeiro "Rio de Janeiro") only.
* January 22: Founding of the first city in Brazil, [São Vicente](/wiki/S%C3%A3o_Vicente,_S%C3%A3o_Paulo "São Vicente, São Paulo"). City of São Vicente only.
* January 25: Founding of [São Paulo](/wiki/S%C3%A3o_Paulo "São Paulo") (Aniversário de São Paulo) and [São Paulo FC](/wiki/S%C3%A3o_Paulo_FC "São Paulo FC"), City of [São Paulo](/wiki/S%C3%A3o_Paulo "São Paulo") only.
* January 26: Founding of [Santos](/wiki/Santos,_S%C3%A3o_Paulo "Santos, São Paulo") (Aniversario de Santos), City of Santos only.
* March 6: [Pernambucan revolt](/wiki/Pernambucan_revolt "Pernambucan revolt"), State of [Pernambuco](/wiki/Pernambuco "Pernambuco") only.
* March 12: Founding of [Recife](/wiki/Recife "Recife") and [Olinda](/wiki/Olinda "Olinda") (Aniversário de Recife e Olinda), Cities of Recife and Olinda only.
* April 14: Founding of [Santos FC](/wiki/Santos_FC "Santos FC"), city of [Santos](/wiki/Santos,_S%C3%A3o_Paulo "Santos, São Paulo") only.
* April 21: Founding of [Brasília](/wiki/Bras%C3%ADlia "Brasília") (Aniversário de Brasília), coinciding with [Tiradentes](/wiki/Tiradentes "Tiradentes"), Federal District only.
* April 23: [Saint George](/wiki/Saint_George "Saint George") (São Jorge), City of Rio de Janeiro only. In 2007, governor [Sérgio Cabral Filho](/wiki/S%C3%A9rgio_Cabral_Filho "Sérgio Cabral Filho") expanded this holiday to all the [State of Rio de Janeiro](/wiki/State_of_Rio_de_Janeiro "State of Rio de Janeiro").
* May 13: [Our Lady of Fátima](/wiki/Our_Lady_of_F%C3%A1tima "Our Lady of Fátima") (Nossa Senhora do Rosário de Fátima) and foundation of [Sport Club do Recife](/wiki/Sport_Club_do_Recife "Sport Club do Recife") (Fundação do Sport Club do Recife), City of [Recife](/wiki/Recife "Recife") only.
* July 2: Bahia Independence Day, State of [Bahia](/wiki/Bahia "Bahia") only.
* July 8: Sergipe Political Emancipation Day, State of [Sergipe](/wiki/Sergipe "Sergipe") only
* July 9: [Constitutionalist Revolution](/wiki/Constitutionalist_Revolution "Constitutionalist Revolution") (Revolução Constitucionalista), [State of São Paulo](/wiki/State_of_S%C3%A3o_Paulo "State of São Paulo") only.
* August 15: Our Lady of the Good Voyage (Nossa Senhora da Boa Viagem), City of [Belo Horizonte](/wiki/Belo_Horizonte "Belo Horizonte") only.
* August 26:
  + Emancipation of the city of Campo Grande, City of [Campo Grande](/wiki/Campo_Grande "Campo Grande") only.
  + Founding of [SE Palmeiras](/wiki/SE_Palmeiras "SE Palmeiras"), City of [São Paulo](/wiki/S%C3%A3o_Paulo "São Paulo") only.
* September 1: Founding of [Sport Club Corinthians Paulista](/wiki/Sport_Club_Corinthians_Paulista "Sport Club Corinthians Paulista") (Fundação do Sport Club Corinthians Paulista), City of [São Paulo](/wiki/S%C3%A3o_Paulo "São Paulo") only.
* September 8: Our Lady of the Light in the Pine (Nossa Senhora da Luz dos Pinhais), City of [Curitiba](/wiki/Curitiba "Curitiba") only.
* September 20: [Farroupilha's Revolution](/wiki/War_of_Tatters "War of Tatters") (Revolução Farroupilha), State of [Rio Grande do Sul](/wiki/Rio_Grande_do_Sul "Rio Grande do Sul") only.
* October 11: Creation of the State of Mato Grosso do Sul, separated from [Mato Grosso](/wiki/Mato_Grosso "Mato Grosso") in 1977, State of [Mato Grosso do Sul](/wiki/Mato_Grosso_do_Sul "Mato Grosso do Sul") only.
* October 3: Uruaçú and Cunhaú Martyrs Day, State of [Rio Grande do Norte](/wiki/Rio_Grande_do_Norte "Rio Grande do Norte") only.
* October 24: Founding of [Goiânia](/wiki/Goi%C3%A2nia "Goiânia"), City of Goiânia only.
* November 4: Founding of [São Carlos](/wiki/S%C3%A3o_Carlos "São Carlos"), City of São Carlos only.
* November 21: Our Lady of Apresentação (Nossa Senhora da Apresentação) Day, City of [Natal](/wiki/Natal,_Rio_Grande_do_Norte "Natal, Rio Grande do Norte") only.
* November 30: [Evangelical](/wiki/Evangelicalism "Evangelicalism") Day (Dia do Evangélico), Federal District only.
* December 8: Our Lady of Conceição (Dia de Nossa Senhora da Conceição), State of [Pernambuco](/wiki/Pernambuco "Pernambuco"), City of [Belo Horizonte](/wiki/Belo_Horizonte "Belo Horizonte"), City of [Dourados](/wiki/Dourados "Dourados"), and Cities of [Santa Maria](/wiki/Santa_Maria,_Rio_Grande_do_Sul "Santa Maria, Rio Grande do Sul") and [São Leopoldo](/wiki/S%C3%A3o_Leopoldo "São Leopoldo") only.
* December 19: Emancipation of the Province of Paraná, State of [Paraná](/wiki/Paran%C3%A1_(state) "Paraná (state)") only.
